name: Darghama ibn Malik
title: The Brave Warrior
shortDescription: A brave Shia from Kufa who, after joining Imam Hussain (AS) in Karbala, was martyred in a heroic hand-to-hand combat.
fullStory:
Darghama ibn Malik al-Taghlibi was a valiant Shia and well-known figure in Kufa. He had great devotion to the Ahl al-Bayt and reached Karbala before Ashura to join Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan. His name means 'fierce lion', and his bravery on the battlefield was a testament to this name.

On the day of Ashura, after the noon prayer, Darghama asked Imam Hussain (AS) for permission to enter the battlefield. He entered the field with a strong body and drawn sword, reciting epic war poetry to announce his nobility and high purpose to the Kufans.

In hand-to-hand combat, Darghama brought down dozens of enemy forces with great skill. His fighting style was so crushing that Umar Sa'd's soldiers feared confronting him directly.

Finally, after a long battle and inflicting fatal blows on the enemy, he was surrounded by cowardly arrow shots and a group attack by the Kufans. While his body was full of wounds, he was martyred defending the son of the Prophet (PBUH).

The name of this brave warrior is also mentioned with greetings and peace from the Imam of the Time (AJ) in the Ziyarat of Nahiya al-Muqaddasa, which indicates his high status before the Ahl al-Bayt.
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Darghama ibn Malik.
